data.title: QuickBooks rated best MTD-compliant platform for UK sole traders by Consumer365. New York, Feb. 20, 2026.
data.description: A recent review by Consumer365 has rated QuickBooks as the leading Making Tax Digital compliant accounting platform for UK sole traders. The evaluation focused on assessing the effectiveness of accounting software in supporting sole traders. The review positioned QuickBooks above competitors, highlighting its compliance with tax regulations and its assistance for small businesses in managing finances. This recognition underscores QuickBooks' popularity among sole traders in the UK, reflecting its strong performance in catering to the specific needs of this demographic.

data.title: Three engineers in Silicon Valley face charges for stealing tech secrets - including mobile processor and cryptography files from Google.
data.description: Three engineers are facing accusations of allegedly stealing confidential mobile processor and cryptography files from Google as well as two other undisclosed companies.

data.title: KuCoin EU expands Austria team for compliance and governance, committed to responsible growth in Europe.
data.description: Vienna, Austria - On February 20, 2026, KuCoin EU Exchange GmbH disclosed their plans to bolster their local compliance and governance team in Austria. This move aligns with their strategy for sustained, regulated expansion in the European market. The company operates under the guidance of the Markets in Crypto-Assets framework.

data.title: cBrain chosen by California for 12-week AI Proof of Concept project, focusing on AI and Natural Language Processing.
data.description: cBrain has been chosen by the State of California to take part in a 12-week Informational Proof of Concept (POC) centered on the utilization of AI and Natural Language Processing technologies.

data.title: Grail stock drops post NHS-Galleri trial; test can detect 50+ cancers early. Market up, stock down. Data showed less Stage IV diagnoses. Johnson Fistel investigates.
data.description: GRAIL Inc. (NASDAQ: GRAL) stock dropped in premarket trading after the NHS-Galleri trial results were announced. The Galleri test is an early cancer detection tool that can identify over 50 types of cancer through a blood draw before symptoms show. While the broader market saw gains, GRAIL's stock declined, possibly due to the trial results. The trial involved 142,000 participants aged 50 to 77 in the NHS, and although the primary endpoint wasn't met, adding Galleri to standard screening led to a significant reduction in late-stage cancer diagnoses. Johnson Fistel is investigating GRAIL to determine if there were any breaches of securities laws in disclosing trial results. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock market stability tested by stalled major indexes despite AI hype, indicating saturation in investors' sentiments.
data.description: In recent months, the main stock market indicators have largely remained stable, with investors becoming less enthusiastic about artificial intelligence.

data.title: A.I. efficiency may lead to more rate cuts, but Fed chair Warsh faces challenge convincing colleagues due to increased productivity potential.
data.description: Kevin M. Warsh, the potential Federal Reserve chair, could have a difficult time convincing his fellow members to reduce interest rates due to the possibility of increased productivity.

data.title: US tourism faces ongoing decline as the only major destination with fewer international visitors last year. Border scrutiny, ICE violence, and policy uncertainty contribute to the slump.
data.description: In the previous year, the United States experienced a decrease in international visitors, marking it as the sole major travel destination with a decline. This drop was influenced by heightened border security measures, instances of violence by ICE, and policies that were deemed erratic. Unfortunately, the trend does not appear to be improving in the new year.
